facts.primaryTitle: The Christmas Doll Opened Her Eyes
facts.setting: The doll arrived without a sender.
criticalEvents: The doll arrived without a sender.
Its card said, 'For the daughter who should have been mine.' Adrian Cole was a widowed father raising his eight-year-old daughter when the story began in a snowbound family home on Christmas Eve.
The first reports did not describe an obvious monster or violent attack.
They described a music-box version of a Christmas carol playing one note too slowly.
facts.threat: The doll arrived without a sender.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did Adrian Cole defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
